file.content:
/* ============================================================================
   @phlix/ui — Density tokens (Nocturne, R0.1)

   [data-density]-scoped sizing for interactive chrome. Default "comfortable"
   on :root; usePreferencesStore (R1) sets [data-density] on <html>. Primitives
   (R0.4) and the grid (R2) read these so a single pref restyles the whole app.

      --control-h     height of buttons / inputs / chips
      --control-pad-x horizontal padding inside controls
      --control-gap   gap between inline controls
      --field-pad-y   vertical padding inside text fields
      --stack-gap     vertical rhythm between stacked rows
   ============================================================================ */
:root,
[data-density='comfortable'] {
  --control-h:     2.5rem;    /* 40px */
  --control-pad-x: 0.875rem;  /* 14px */
  --control-gap:   0.5rem;    /* 8px  */
  --field-pad-y:   0.75rem;   /* 12px */
  --stack-gap:     1rem;      /* 16px */
}

[data-density='compact'] {
  --control-h:     2.125rem;  /* 34px */
  --control-pad-x: 0.625rem;  /* 10px */
  --control-gap:   0.375rem;  /* 6px  */
  --field-pad-y:   0.5rem;    /* 8px  */
  --stack-gap:     0.625rem;  /* 10px */
}

    "./css/*": "./dist/css/*",

    "./package.json": "./package.json"

  "files": [
    "dist",
    "src/css"
  ],

    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json",
    "test": "vitest",
    "test:run": "vitest run",
    "lint": "eslint ."
  },

  },
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=18.0.0"
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "github:detain/phlix-tokens"

  "keywords": [
    "phlix",

    "theme",
    "css-variables"
  ],
  "license": "MIT"
}

# @phlix/tokens — CI
#
# Deterministic gate: regenerate tokens (must be a no-op vs the committed
# artifact), typecheck, build, and run the full vitest suite. Manual dispatch by
# default (no runner wired to this repo yet); uncomment the triggers to make it
# a required PR check.
name: tokens-ci

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  # pull_request:
  #   branches: [master]
  # push:
  #   branches: [master]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '24'
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      # The generated tokens must match what's committed — fail the build if the
      # CSS changed without regenerating.
      - run: npm run generate
      - name: Verify generated tokens are up to date
        run: git diff --exit-code -- src/tokens.generated.ts src/tokens.generated.json
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npm run typecheck
      - run: npm run build
      - run: npm run test:run

# @phlix/tokens

Framework-agnostic **design tokens** for Phlix — the single source of truth for
theme tokens shared across every client so they never drift:

- **Web** clients (phlix-ui, windows, tizen) consume the shipped CSS custom
  properties.
- **Non-CSS** clients (React Native mobile, Roku) consume the resolved JS/JSON
  token maps.

It has **no dependency on Vue, Pinia, or vue-router**, so it is safe to import
from a React Native bundle.

The authoritative web artifact is the CSS in [`src/css/`](./src/css), copied
byte-for-byte from phlix-ui's `src/tokens/`. The JS token objects in
`src/tokens.generated.ts` are **generated** from that CSS (var() references
resolved to concrete values per theme), guaranteeing zero visual drift.

## Install

```sh
npm i github:detain/phlix-tokens
```

## Usage — web (CSS variables)

Import the self-contained stylesheet once at app entry. It declares the token
custom properties scoped to `:root` and the `[data-theme=…]` / `[data-density=…]`
blocks:

```ts
import '@phlix/tokens/style.css';
```

Then select a theme/density on `<html>` (or any element) — either by setting the
attributes yourself or via the helper:

```ts
import { applyTokenAttributes } from '@phlix/tokens';

applyTokenAttributes(document.documentElement, {
  theme: 'nocturne',     // 'nocturne' | 'daylight' | 'midnight'
  density: 'comfortable',// 'comfortable' | 'compact'
  reducedMotion: false,
  accent: null,          // or a hex string to override the default amber
});
```

Individual token files are also exported if you want to import a subset:

```ts
import '@phlix/tokens/css/colors.css';
import '@phlix/tokens/css/spacing.css';
```

## Usage — React Native / Roku (resolved values)

There are no CSS variables in React Native, so read the **flat resolved map** for
a theme. Every `var(--ref)` is already resolved to a concrete value;
`clamp()` / `rgba()` strings are preserved as-is.

```ts
import { tokens, resolveTheme } from '@phlix/tokens';

const t = resolveTheme('nocturne');
t['--bg'];     // '#0b0a08'
t['--accent']; // '#f5a524'
t['--text'];   // '#f3ece1'

tokens.spacing['--space-4'];   // '1rem'
tokens.radius['--radius-lg'];  // '14px'
tokens.density.compact['--control-h']; // '2.125rem'
```

The same data is also available as JSON:

```ts
import data from '@phlix/tokens/tokens.json';
```

### Deriving a custom accent

`deriveAccentVars(hex)` produces the full accent role set (hover/active/soft/
ring/contrast) from a single hex — the same pure logic phlix-ui's accent picker
uses:

```ts
import { deriveAccentVars } from '@phlix/tokens';

deriveAccentVars('#3366ff');
// { '--accent': '#3366ff', '--accent-hover': …, '--accent-contrast': '#fff8ec', … }
```

## Regenerating tokens

The JS token objects are generated from `src/css/*.css`. After editing the CSS,
regenerate the committed `src/tokens.generated.{ts,json}`:

```sh
npm run generate
```

The generator is pure and deterministic (no network, no clock/random). CI fails
if the committed artifact is out of date.

## Scripts

| script           | purpose                                                     |
| ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `npm run generate` | parse CSS → write `src/tokens.generated.{ts,json}`        |
| `npm run typecheck`| `tsc --noEmit` (strict)                                   |
| `npm run build`    | generate → typecheck → vite lib build (ES+CJS) → d.ts → CSS |
| `npm run test`     | vitest (watch)                                            |
| `npm run test:run` | vitest run (once)                                         |
| `npm run lint`     | eslint                                                    |

## License

MIT

# Changelog

All notable changes to this project are documented here. The format is based on
[Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/), and this project
adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [0.1.0] - 2026-06-26

### Added

- Initial release: framework-agnostic design tokens for Phlix, extracted from
  phlix-ui so web and non-CSS clients share one source of truth.
- Shipped CSS custom properties in `src/css/` (colors, spacing, radius, shadow,
  motion, density, typography, index), copied byte-for-byte from phlix-ui's
  `src/tokens/`. Self-contained `@phlix/tokens/style.css` plus per-file
  `@phlix/tokens/css/*` exports.
- `tokens` object and `resolveTheme(name)` — resolved JS token maps (var()
  references resolved to concrete values per theme; `clamp()`/`rgba()` strings
  preserved) for React Native / Roku and other non-CSS consumers. Also emitted
  as `@phlix/tokens/tokens.json`.

- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-tokens/vite.config.ts` — lib mode ES+CJS, NO Vue plugin, vitest `environment: node`.
- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-tokens/eslint.config.js` — flat TS config (`no-explicit-any: error`).

  — copied BYTE-IDENTICAL from phlix-ui/src/tokens (verified via `diff -q`, no diffs).
- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-tokens/src/accent.ts` — ported verbatim from phlix-ui `src/composables/color.ts`
  (`deriveAccentVars`, `parseHex`/`toHex`/`lighten`/`darken`/`rgba`/`luminance`, `RGB`) + `ACCENT_KEYS`.

  `DEFAULT_THEME`/`DEFAULT_DENSITY`, `DATA_THEME`/`DATA_DENSITY`/`DATA_REDUCED_MOTION`, pure
  `applyTokenAttributes()` (DOM-guarded via `typeof document`; mirrors phlix-ui `applyToRoot`, no Vue).
- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-tokens/src/index.ts` — main entry, re-exports tokens/types/accent/theme helpers.
- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-tokens/src/tokens.generated.ts` + `.json` — GENERATED (committed).
- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-tokens/scripts/generate-tokens.mjs` — pure/deterministic CSS→tokens generator.
- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-tokens/scripts/build-css.mjs` — copies css to dist/css + inlines index.css's
  @imports into a self-contained dist/style.css.
- `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-tokens/test/tokens.test.ts` — vitest suite.

### Dep versions pulled from phlix-ui/package.json (exact)
- typescript `^6.0.3`
- vite `^8.0.16`
- vitest `^4.1.8`
- @vitest/coverage-v8 `^4.1.8`
- @types/node `^25.9.1`
- eslint `^9.39.0` + @eslint/js `^9.39.0` + typescript-eslint `^8.46.0` (phlix-ui uses eslint via different
  plugins for .vue; this package is plain TS so I used the standard typescript-eslint flat config — these
  three eslint versions are NOT from phlix-ui, they are current-stable picks; coordinator may pin to
  whatever `npm install` resolves).

### Decisions / assumptions
- `deriveAccentVars` actually lives in phlix-ui `src/composables/color.ts` (useTheme.ts imports it from
  `./color`); ported from there. `ACCENT_KEYS` (6 keys) taken from useTheme.ts and equals the keys
  `deriveAccentVars` returns.
- Generator selector model: `:root`→base, `[data-theme='x']`→theme, `[data-density='y']`→density.
  `var(--ref)` resolved within scope (base, or base∪theme); cross-file refs resolve (e.g.
  `--accent`→`--amber-500`→`#f5a524`; `--text-on-accent`→`--accent-contrast`→`#2a1804`;
  `--fw-bold`→`--font-bold`→`700`). clamp()/rgba() preserved verbatim. index.css skipped (only @imports
  + reset rules, no token decls). Counts: base 140, each theme 64 own vars.
- `tokens.shadow` is surfaced from the Nocturne theme block (shadows are theme-scoped, not in `:root`),
  giving non-CSS consumers the default ladder. `tokens.density` = `{comfortable, compact}`.
- `resolveTheme(name)` = flat `base ∪ theme` resolved map; returns a fresh copy each call.
- Tests use `environment: node` (no jsdom dep) with an in-memory `TokenTarget` stub + a `globalThis.document`
  stub for the documentElement-fallback path.
- `style.css` self-containment: `scripts/build-css.mjs` inlines index.css's relative @imports into
  `dist/style.css` AND copies each file to `dist/css/` so both `@phlix/tokens/style.css` and
  `@phlix/tokens/css/*` work. Verified locally: 0 @imports remain in built style.css, 8 files copied.
- exports map: `.`→es/cjs+types, `./style.css`→dist/style.css, `./css/*`→dist/css/*,
  `./tokens.json`→src/tokens.generated.json. `files`: ["dist","src/css"].

### Verified locally (allowed: node script that authors the committed artifact)
- `node scripts/generate-tokens.mjs` runs clean; ALL var() chains resolve (no `var(` left in any flat
  theme/base/density map).
- `node scripts/build-css.mjs` produces self-contained dist/style.css (then removed dist/ — gitignored).
- CSS copies are byte-identical to phlix-ui source (`diff -q`).

### Coordinator must run (I did NOT)
1. `npm install` in `/home/sites/phlix/phlix-tokens`.
2. `npm run generate` then `git diff --exit-code src/tokens.generated.*` (must be a no-op — already committed).
3. `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, `npm run test:run`.
4. `npm run lint`.
- Possible install-time pin: the three eslint deps (eslint/@eslint/js/typescript-eslint) are best-guess
  versions; if `npm install` complains, pin to resolved versions.
